cross_category_overview: Across every category, regulators are shifting from rule-setting to active enforcement — ASIC, AHPRA, TEQSA, AER, AMSA, AFP and eSafety are all visibly escalating investigations, bans and penalties — while industry-wide pressure for a 25% red tape reduction collides with new compliance layers (NDIS reform, AML/CTF Tranche 2, DISP, digital asset and online safety regimes), creating concentrated advisory demand around enforcement readiness, accountability frameworks (FAR-style, director, practitioner) and integrated compliance uplift. The cross-cutting opportunity for KPMG sits at the intersection of deregulation impact assessments and heightened conduct/supply-chain/fraud controls, particularly where digital platforms, foreign investment structuring, and sovereign/AUKUS-aligned obligations are reshaping risk for both public sector and regulated industry clients.

hub_wide.brief: Three regulator investment themes dominate this fortnight: AER is funding a sustained NSW transmission cost-assurance build-out around Transgrid's System Strength Project 2026-31 (Project Owner's Cost Assessment, Non-Contestable Risk Cost Allowance methodology, 2026-27 distributor pricing approvals); CASA is simultaneously opening RPAS-over-people (PP 2609US) and uniquely-Australian structural fatigue AD consultations alongside Part 138/133 surveillance and Bankstown airspace redesign work; and ATSB/AMSA are visibly scaling safety-investigation and crew-welfare enforcement capacity, headlined by the six-month vessel ban of a Chinese-owned bulker for seafarer underpayments and a cluster of E190, Cessna, LongRanger and Melbourne runway investigations. Named clients and counterparties in the news include Transgrid, NSW distributors, China Airlines, Embraer/E190 operators, and the Chinese-owned bulker operator sanctioned by AMSA, with WA Main Roads ($339.9m road safety injection) and Transport for NSW Regional Rail framing the state capex pipeline. Insurance Council of Australia's push on Queensland e-mobility CTP reform and NSW Point-to-Point on-street compliance round out a fortnight where regulator capability spend is converging on cost assurance, safety management and conduct enforcement. KPMG should invest BD effort now in three lanes: a Transgrid/REZ regulatory-economics and non-contestable risk allowance assurance offer ahead of AER's determination; a CASA consultation-response and RPAS/structural-fatigue impact-modelling capability for operators and OEMs; and an SMS/human-factors and seafarer-welfare assurance package for aviation and maritime operators exposed to ATSB and AMSA enforcement. Secondary plays sit around WA and NSW road and rail program assurance, and an ICA-aligned CTP and e-mobility scheme-design advisory line into Queensland Treasury.
implications: Stand up a Transgrid System Strength Project 2026-31 cost-assurance and Non-Contestable Risk Cost Allowance review offer this fortnight, before AER locks in methodology.
Mobilise the aviation regulatory team to lead client CASA submissions on PP 2609US (RPAS over people) and the structural fatigue AD review, packaged with an impact-modelling deliverable.

Pitch an SMS, human-factors and seafarer-welfare assurance diagnostic to tier-1 aviation and maritime operators on the back of the AMSA six-month vessel ban and the E190/LongRanger/Melbourne ATSB investigation cluster.

client_news_weekly.brief: VIC cluster is light this week, with a single but partner-relevant item: Maddocks' analysis of Victoria's proposed Occupational Health & Safety reforms. WorkSafe Victoria will face uplift expectations on regulator readiness, employer guidance and enforcement analytics — a clear pull-through for PEPI's regulatory operating-model and assurance offerings, and a hook into DTF/DGS on cross-portfolio compliance cost. Federal activity is dominated by aviation safety and energy regulation. CASA has opened consultation on RPAS (drone) operations over or near people (PP 2609US) and released a Part 133 plain English guide and Part 138 surveillance update — consultation windows are an immediate BD opening for regulatory design, RIS and stakeholder engagement support. ATSB published multiple investigation outcomes (E190 Cairns landing gear overspeed, Cessna 182 fuel exhaustion, Melbourne runway excursion safety actions) reinforcing demand for safety management system assurance. AER approved 2026–27 electricity distributor pricing and released Transgrid System Strength Project 2026–31 cost assessment, risk allowance methodology and NSW REZ revenue adjustments — strong pull-through for regulatory economics, cost assurance and PMO. ASIC's finfluencer expansion, ACCC's $15m Emma Sleep penalty, NDIA legislative amendments (MinterEllison) and three ASPI Defence pieces (UK naval posture, northern systems, National Defence Strategy 2026) round out integrity, consumer protection and Defence capability themes. Cross-cluster: regulator capability uplift, consultation-driven policy design, and infrastructure cost assurance are the dominant BD vectors — prioritise CASA, AER and WorkSafe outreach this fortnight.
